name: AMD ROCm
description: AMD ROCm (Radeon Open Compute) is a software stack for GPU programming on AMD GPUs. It provides a comprehensive development platform for high-performance computing and machine learning applications.

notes.text: Execute within an existing LXC Console
Supported OS: Debian 12, Debian 13, Ubuntu 22.04, Ubuntu 24.04
Requires GPU passthrough to the LXC container before installation
GPU passthrough: Find the GID from 'ls -la /dev/dri/renderD128' on host, then add to as a new device to /etc/pve/lxc/<CTID>.conf: dev<#>: /dev/kfd,gid=<GID>
After installation, run 'source /etc/profile.d/rocm.sh' or log out/in to update PATH
Verify installation with: rocminfo and rocm-smi commands
If rocminfo shows 'Unable to open /dev/kfd', GPU passthrough is not configured correctly
